english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Hebrews

verses.latin_vulgate: Unde, fratres sancti, vocationis cælestis participes, considerate Apostolum, et pontificem confessionis nostræ Jesum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅθεν ἀδελφοὶ ἅγιοι κλήσεως ἐπουρανίου μέτοχοι κατανοήσατε τὸν ἀπόστολον καὶ ἀρχιερέα τῆς ὁμολογίας ἡμῶν χριστὸν ἰησοῦν
verses.king_james: Wherefore, holy brethren, partakers of the heavenly calling, consider the Apostle and High Priest of our profession, Christ Jesus;

verses.latin_vulgate: qui fidelis est ei, qui fecit illum, sicut et Moyses in omni domo ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πιστὸν ὄντα τῷ ποιήσαντι αὐτὸν ὡς καὶ μωσῆς ἐν ὅλῳ τῷ οἴκῳ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Who was faithful to him that appointed him, as also Moses *was faithful* in all his house.

verses.latin_vulgate: Amplioris enim gloriæ iste præ Moyse dignus est habitus, quanto ampliorem honorem habet domus, qui fabricavit illam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πλείονος γὰρ δόξης οὗτος παρὰ μωσῆν ἠξίωται καθ᾽ ὅσον πλείονα τιμὴν ἔχει τοῦ οἴκου ὁ κατασκευάσας αὐτόν
verses.king_james: For this *man* was counted worthy of more glory than Moses, inasmuch as he who hath builded the house hath more honour than the house.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnis namque domus fabricatur ab aliquo: qui autem omnia creavit, Deus est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πᾶς γὰρ οἶκος κατασκευάζεται ὑπό τινος ὁ δὲ τὰ πάντα κατασκευάσας θεός
verses.king_james: For every house is builded by some *man*; but he that built all things *is* God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et Moyses quidem fidelis erat in tota domo ejus tamquam famulus, in testimonium eorum, quæ dicenda erant:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ μωσῆς μὲν πιστὸς ἐν ὅλῳ τῷ οἴκῳ αὐτοῦ ὡς θεράπων εἰς μαρτύριον τῶν λαληθησομένων
verses.king_james: And Moses verily *was* faithful in all his house, as a servant, for a testimony of those things which were to be spoken after;

verses.latin_vulgate: Christus vero tamquam filius in domo sua: quæ domus sumus nos, si fiduciam, et gloriam spei usque ad finem, firmam retineamus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: χριστὸς δὲ ὡς υἱὸς ἐπὶ τὸν οἶκον αὐτοῦ οὗ οἶκός ἐσμεν ἡμεῖς ἐάνπερ τὴν παῤῥησίαν καὶ τὸ καύχημα τῆς ἐλπίδος μέχρι τέλους βεβαίαν κατάσχωμεν
verses.king_james: But Christ as a son over his own house; whose house are we, if we hold fast the confidence and the rejoicing of the hope firm unto the end.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quapropter sicut dicit Spiritus Sanctus: Hodie si vocem ejus audieritis,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διό καθὼς λέγει τὸ πνεῦμα τὸ ἅγιον σήμερον ἐὰν τῆς φωνῆς αὐτοῦ ἀκούσητε
verses.king_james: Wherefore (as the Holy Ghost saith, To day if ye will hear his voice,

verses.latin_vulgate: nolite obdurare corda vestra, sicut in exacerbatione secundum diem tentationis in deserto,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ σκληρύνητε τὰς καρδίας ὑμῶν ὡς ἐν τῷ παραπικρασμῷ κατὰ τὴν ἡμέραν τοῦ πειρασμοῦ ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ
verses.king_james: Harden not your hearts, as in the provocation, in the day of temptation in the wilderness:

verses.latin_vulgate: ubi tentaverunt me patres vestri: probaverunt, et viderunt opera mea
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὗ ἐπείρασαν με οἱ πατέρες ὑμῶν ἐδοκιμασάν με, καὶ εἶδον τὰ ἔργα μου τεσσαράκοντα ἔτη
verses.king_james: When your fathers tempted me, proved me, and saw my works forty years.

verses.latin_vulgate: quadraginta annis: propter quod infensus fui generationi huic, et dixi: Semper errant corde. Ipsi autem non cognoverunt vias meas,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὸ προσώχθισα τῇ γενεᾷ ἐκείνῃ, καὶ εἶπον ἀεὶ πλανῶνται τῇ καρδίᾳ αὐτοὶ δὲ οὐκ ἔγνωσαν τὰς ὁδούς μου
verses.king_james: Wherefore I was grieved with that generation, and said, They do alway err in *their* heart; and they have not known my ways.

verses.latin_vulgate: sicut juravi in ira mea: Si introibunt in requiem meam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς ὤμοσα ἐν τῇ ὀργῇ μου εἰ εἰσελεύσονται εἰς τὴν κατάπαυσίν μου
verses.king_james: So I sware in my wrath, They shall not enter into my rest.)

verses.latin_vulgate: Videte fratres, ne forte sit in aliquo vestrum cor malum incredulitatis, discedendi a Deo vivo:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: βλέπετε ἀδελφοί μήποτε ἔσται ἔν τινι ὑμῶν καρδία πονηρὰ ἀπιστίας ἐν τῷ ἀποστῆναι ἀπὸ θεοῦ ζῶντος
verses.king_james: Take heed, brethren, lest there be in any of you an evil heart of unbelief, in departing from the living God.

verses.latin_vulgate: sed adhortamini vosmetipsos per singulos dies, donec hodie cognominatur, ut non obduretur quis ex vobis fallacia peccati.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλὰ παρακαλεῖτε ἑαυτοὺς καθ᾽ ἑκάστην ἡμέραν ἄχρις οὗ τὸ σήμερον καλεῖται ἵνα μὴ σκληρυνθῇ τις ἐξ ὑμῶν ἀπάτῃ τῆς ἁμαρτίας
verses.king_james: But exhort one another daily, while it is called To day; lest any of you be hardened through the deceitfulness of sin.

verses.latin_vulgate: Participes enim Christi effecti sumus, si tamen initium substantiæ ejus usque ad finem firmum retineamus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μέτοχοι γὰρ γεγόναμεν τοῦ χριστοῦ ἐάνπερ τὴν ἀρχὴν τῆς ὑποστάσεως μέχρι τέλους βεβαίαν κατάσχωμεν
verses.king_james: For we are made partakers of Christ, if we hold the beginning of our confidence stedfast unto the end;

verses.latin_vulgate: Dum dicitur: Hodie si vocem ejus audieritis, nolite obdurare corda vestra, quemadmodum in illa exacerbatione.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν τῷ λέγεσθαι σήμερον ἐὰν τῆς φωνῆς αὐτοῦ ἀκούσητε μὴ σκληρύνητε τὰς καρδίας ὑμῶν ὡς ἐν τῷ παραπικρασμῷ
verses.king_james: While it is said, To day if ye will hear his voice, harden not your hearts, as in the provocation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quidam enim audientes exacerbaverunt: sed non universi qui profecti sunt ex Ægypto per Moysen.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τίνες γὰρ ἀκούσαντες παρεπίκραναν ἀλλ᾽ οὐ πάντες οἱ ἐξελθόντες ἐξ αἰγύπτου διὰ μωσέως
verses.king_james: For some, when they had heard, did provoke: howbeit not all that came out of Egypt by Moses.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quibus autem infensus est quadraginta annis? nonne illis qui peccaverunt, quorum cadavera prostrata sunt in deserto?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τίσιν δὲ προσώχθισεν τεσσαράκοντα ἔτη οὐχὶ τοῖς ἁμαρτήσασιν ὧν τὰ κῶλα ἔπεσεν ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ
verses.king_james: But with whom was he grieved forty years? *was it* not with them that had sinned, whose carcases fell in the wilderness?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quibus autem juravit non introire in requiem ipsius, nisi illis qui increduli fuerunt?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τίσιν δὲ ὤμοσεν μὴ εἰσελεύσεσθαι εἰς τὴν κατάπαυσιν αὐτοῦ εἰ μὴ τοῖς ἀπειθήσασιν
verses.king_james: And to whom sware he that they should not enter into his rest, but to them that believed not?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et videmus, quia non potuerunt introire propter incredulitatem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ βλέπομεν ὅτι οὐκ ἠδυνήθησαν εἰσελθεῖν δι᾽ ἀπιστίαν
verses.king_james: So we see that they could not enter in because of unbelief.
